name: bash
context: Execute Bash commands, use builtins, perform expansion and globbing, and handle command output
info: Bash (Bourne Again SHell) is the primary command interpreter on most Linux systems and the default execution environment for AI agents. This skill covers Bash command execution, builtin commands (cd, echo, printf, read, test, type, source, eval, exec), variable expansion ($var, ${var:-default}, ${var##pattern}), arithmetic ($(( ))), globbing (*, ?, []), brace expansion ({a,b,c}, {1..10}), command substitution ($(command)), and conditional expressions ([[ ]]). Agents use Bash to run tools, inspect systems, transform data, and orchestrate multi-step workflows. See [[shell/environment]] for variable management, [[shell/pipes]] for data flow, and [[shell/scripting]] for control structures.
body:
# Bash Command Execution

Bash is the execution engine for agents. Every tool invocation, file operation, and system query flows through Bash commands.

## Command Execution

Bash executes commands by searching `$PATH` for executables, then running them in a child process:

```bash
# Simple command
ls -la /tmp

# Command with captured output
result=$(git status --porcelain)

# Command with exit code check
if git diff --quiet; then
  echo "No changes"
fi

# Command chain — all must succeed
mkdir -p build && cd build && cmake .. && make

# Command list — run regardless of failures
rm -f old.log; touch new.log; echo "Reset complete"
```

## Builtin Commands

Builtins execute inside the Bash process (no child process). Key builtins:

| Builtin | Purpose | Example |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `cd` | Change directory | `cd /var/log` |
| `echo` | Print to stdout | `echo "hello $USER"` |
| `printf` | Formatted output | `printf '%s\n' "$var"` |
| `read` | Read stdin into variable | `read -r line < file.txt` |
| `test` / `[` | Evaluate conditionals | `[ -f file.txt ] && echo exists` |
| `type` | Show command type | `type -a python` |
| `source` / `.` | Execute file in current shell | `source .env` |
| `export` | Set env var for children | `export PATH="$HOME/bin:$PATH"` |
| `eval` | Evaluate string as command | `eval "$cmd"` (use sparingly) |
| `exec` | Replace shell with command | `exec python app.py` |
| `set` | Set shell options | `set -euo pipefail` |
| `trap` | Register signal handlers | `trap cleanup EXIT` |
| `shift` | Shift positional parameters | `shift 2` |
| `return` | Return from function | `return 1` |
| `exit` | Exit shell with code | `exit 0` |

## Variable Expansion

Bash expands variables before command execution:

```bash
# Basic expansion
echo "$HOME"            # /home/user
echo "${HOME}"          # Same, explicit braces

# Default values
echo "${PORT:-8080}"    # Use 8080 if PORT is unset/empty
echo "${PORT:=8080}"    # Same, but also sets PORT

# Error on unset
echo "${API_KEY:?API_KEY must be set}"  # Exits if unset

# String manipulation
path="/home/user/file.tar.gz"
echo "${path##*/}"       # file.tar.gz (remove longest prefix */)
echo "${path%.gz}"       # /home/user/file.tar (remove shortest suffix .gz)
echo "${path%%.*}"       # /home/user/file (remove longest suffix .*)
echo "${#path}"          # 25 (string length)
echo "${path/user/dev}" # /home/dev/file.tar.gz (substitution)
```

## Arithmetic

```bash
# Arithmetic expansion
echo $(( 2 + 3 ))       # 5
echo $(( count++ ))      # Increment
echo $(( base * 2 ))     # Multiply

# Arithmetic comparison
if (( count > 10 )); then
  echo "Over limit"
fi

# C-style for loop
for (( i=0; i<10; i++ )); do
  echo "$i"
done
```

## Globbing

```bash
# Star — match any string
ls *.js              # All .js files
ls src/**/*.ts       # Recursive (needs shopt -s globstar)

# Question mark — match single character
ls file?.txt         # file1.txt, fileA.txt

# Brackets — match character class
ls file[0-9].txt     # file0.txt through file9.txt
ls file[!0-9].txt    # file not followed by digit

# Brace expansion (NOT globbing — generated before filesystem check)
echo {a,b,c}.txt     # a.txt b.txt c.txt
echo file{1..5}.txt  # file1.txt file2.txt file3.txt file4.txt file5.txt
mkdir -p src/{components,utils,hooks}
```

## Conditional Expressions

```bash
# [[ ]] — extended test (Bash-specific, preferred)
[[ -f "$file" ]]         # File exists and is regular file
[[ -d "$dir" ]]          # Directory exists
[[ -z "$var" ]]          # String is empty
[[ -n "$var" ]]          # String is non-empty
[[ "$a" == "$b" ]]       # String equality
[[ "$a" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]   # Regex match
[[ "$a" < "$b" ]]        # Lexicographic comparison

# Combine conditions
[[ -f "$f" && -r "$f" ]]  # File exists AND is readable
[[ -f "$f" || -L "$f" ]]  # File exists OR is symlink
```

## Command Substitution

```bash
# Preferred: $() — nestable, clearer
files=$(find . -name '*.py' -type f)
count=$(echo "$files" | wc -l)

# Legacy: backticks — avoid, harder to nest
files=`find . -name '*.py' -type f`
```

## Useful Patterns for Agents

```bash
# Check if command exists
if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
  echo "jq is available"
fi

# Temporary file with cleanup
tmp=$(mktemp)
trap "rm -f $tmp" EXIT
command > "$tmp"

# Read file line by line
while IFS= read -r line; do
  echo "Processing: $line"
done < input.txt

# Parse key=value pairs
while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
  export "$key=$value"
done < .env
```

sections.title: Bash builtins
sections.body: Builtins run inside the current shell process — they can modify shell state (cd changes directory, export sets environment). Key builtins: `cd`, `echo`, `printf`, `read`, `test`/`[`, `type`, `source`/`.`, `export`, `eval`, `exec`, `set`, `trap`, `shift`, `return`, `exit`, `declare`, `local`, `readonly`, `unset`, `alias`, `hash`, `help`, `history`, `jobs`, `kill`, `wait`, `bg`, `fg`, `disown`, `pushd`, `popd`, `dirs`. Use `type -a command` to check if something is a builtin, alias, or external command.

sections.title: Variable and parameter expansion
sections.body: Bash expansion order: brace → tilde → parameter/variable → command substitution → arithmetic → word splitting → pathname (glob). Key expansions: `${var:-default}` (default if unset), `${var:=default}` (assign default), `${var:?error}` (error if unset), `${var:+alternate}` (alternate if set), `${#var}` (length), `${var#pattern}` (remove shortest prefix), `${var##pattern}` (longest prefix), `${var%pattern}` (shortest suffix), `${var%%pattern}` (longest suffix), `${var/old/new}` (substitute first), `${var//old/new}` (substitute all).

sections.title: Globbing and brace expansion
sections.body: Globbing matches filenames: `*` (any string), `?` (one char), `[abc]` (character class), `[!abc]` (negated class). Extended globs (`shopt -s extglob`): `?(pat)` (zero or one), `*(pat)` (zero or more), `+(pat)` (one or more), `@(pat)` (exactly one), `!(pat)` (none). Recursive glob (`shopt -s globstar`): `**/*.js` matches at any depth. Brace expansion is NOT globbing — it's generated before filesystem matching: `{a,b}` expands to `a b`, `{1..5}` to `1 2 3 4 5`, `{01..10..2}` to `01 03 05 07 09`.

sections.title: Conditional expressions
sections.body: Use `[[ ]]` for tests in Bash (safer than `[ ]` — no word splitting, supports `&&`, `||`, `=~`). File tests: `-f` (regular file), `-d` (directory), `-e` (exists), `-r`/`-w`/`-x` (readable/writable/executable), `-s` (non-empty), `-L` (symlink), `-nt`/`-ot` (newer/older than). String tests: `-z` (empty), `-n` (non-empty), `==` (equal), `!=` (not equal), `=~` (regex match), `<`/`>` (lexicographic). Integer comparison: use `(( ))` arithmetic or `-eq`, `-ne`, `-lt`, `-gt`, `-le`, `-ge` inside `[[ ]]`.
